en.extract:
The green-headed oriole (Oriolus chlorocephalus), or montane oriole, is a species of bird in the family Oriolidae. It is found in eastern Africa.


Taxonomy and systematics


Subspecies
Three subspecies are recognized: 

O. c. amani - Benson, 1946: Found in south-eastern Kenya and eastern Tanzania
O. c. chlorocephalus - Shelley, 1896: Found in Malawi and central Mozambique
O. c. speculifer - Clancey, 1969: Found in southern Mozambique


Distribution and habitat
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical, dry lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.


Behaviour and ecology
They feed on fruit, seeds, nectar and insects or insect larvae.


Gallery


References


External links
 Media related to Oriolus chlorocephalus at Wikimedia Commons
 Data related to Oriolus chlorocephalus at Wikispecies

The '''green-headed oriole''' ('''''Oriolus chlorocephalus'''''), or '''montane oriole''', is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Oriolidae]]. It is found in eastern Africa.

==Taxonomy and systematics==
===Subspecies===
Three [[subspecies]] are recognized:<ref>{{Cite journal|title=IOC World Bird List 7.1|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref|journal=IOC World Bird List Datasets|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.7.1|doi-access=free}}</ref> 
* ''O. c. amani'' - <small>[[Constantine Walter Benson|Benson]], 1946</small>: Found in south-eastern Kenya and eastern Tanzania
* ''O. c. chlorocephalus'' - <small>[[George Ernest Shelley|Shelley]], 1896</small>: Found in Malawi and central Mozambique
* ''O. c. speculifer'' - <small>[[Phillip Clancey|Clancey]], 1969</small>: Found in southern Mozambique

==Distribution and habitat==
Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical, dry lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.

==Behaviour and ecology==
They feed on fruit, seeds, nectar and insects or insect larvae.<ref name="hbw">{{cite web |last1=Walther |first1=B. |last2=Jones |first2=P. |title=Green-headed Oriole (Oriolus chlorocephalus) |url=https://www.hbw.com/node/60454|website=Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive|publisher=Lynx Edicions, Barcelona | date = 2016 |access-date=22 September 2016}}</ref>
